name: Student Organization Accounts Services
summary:
Student Organization Accounts Service (SOAS) provides financial services to student organizations and acts as the liaison on behalf of student organizations with campus partners.

 Our office hours are Monday-Friday 9:00 am - 5:00 pm.

name: Center for Campus Involvement
summary: We register and support over 1400 student organizations and coordinate over 300 programs per year!

name: Society of Women Engineers
summary: The Society of Women Engineers at the University of Michigan encourages women to excel as engineers and leaders and promotes an inclusive community within the College and the profession that fosters universal success in engineering.

name: Retail and Luxury Goods Club
summary: The RLGC'S mission is to create a space for retail enthusiasts + those curious, to learn more about the ins and outs of the industry and gain insight into the variety of professional opportunities that exist.

name: Interfraternity Council
summary: The Interfraternity Council (IFC) is the local governing body for National Interfraternity Conference fraternity chapters at the University of Michigan.

name: Lambda Phi Epsilon
summary: Lambda Phi Epsilon is an internationally-recognized Asian-interest fraternity in the United States and Canada. Being the largest Asian-interest fraternity, Lambda Phi Epsilon continues to expand to other campuses every year.

name: Engineering Global Leadership Honors Society
summary: The mission of the Engineering Global Leadership Honors Program is to develop the personal, professional, and academic capabilities of our members through engineering excellence, business acumen, cultural awareness, and leadership development.

name: Intellectual Property Student Association
summary: The Intellectual Property Student Association aims to develop and further the community of law students interested in practice areas related to intellectual property by organizing academic, career-related, and social events.

name: Men's Rowing
summary: We are the 14-year American Collegiate Rowing Association National Champions. We are dedicated to representing the University of Michigan by competing against, and beating, the top rowing teams in the nation.

name: Boxing at the University of Michigan
summary: Boxing team which aims at developing competitive fighters to represent the organization within the USIBA and local tournaments.

name: Energy Club at Ross
summary: The Energy Club at Ross (ECR) is a group of primarily graduate students who share an interest in energy. ECR provides opportunities to explore the rapidly-changing energy landscape, while gaining exposure to career opportunities in the industry.

name: Michigan Business Women (BBA)
summary: Michigan Business Women supports and empowers women in business through professional, social, and philanthropic events.

name: German Club at the University of Michigan
summary: German Club's mission is to unite students with a passion for German culture and language and to promote the spread of German culture across campus, especially in the form of cross-cultural exchanges with other language clubs.

name: Men's Club Ice Hockey
summary: The University of Michigan Men's Club Ice Hockey Team competes in the Division II American Collegiate Hockey Association (ACHA).

name: TEDxUofM
summary: TEDxUofM aims to galvanize the University of Michigan community by bringing together passionate individuals across all disciplines who believe in TED's motto of "Ideas Change Everything."

name: Michigan Mars Rover Team
summary: The Michigan Mars Rover Team (MRover) is a student-run, multidisciplinary organization whose mission is to design, build, and test a rover for the exploration of Mars-like environments and to assist astronauts on a mission to Mars.

name: MRun – Michigan Running Club
summary: The Michigan Running Club (MRun, as we call ourselves) is a very active club that offers more than just running. In addition to daily practices, cross country meets, and track meets, we also have social events and charity events throughout each semester.

name: Michigan Student Artificial Intelligence Lab
summary: Michigan Student Artificial Intelligence Lab promotes student interest and participation in AI research, application, and development at U of M.

name: Enactus
summary: Enactus is a Ross-based undergraduate consulting club that conducts pro-bono projects with an emphasis in sustainability and social impact with local and global clients. With a diverse family of 40 members and 10 majors, we would love to have you to

name: Student Funded Fellowships
summary: SFF raises money to fund first year law students doing unpaid public interest work in their first summer of law school. All funding decisions are made through a competitive grant process.

name: Asian Pacific American Law Students Association
summary: The Asian Pacific American Law Students Association (APALSA) serves as an academic, social, and political resource for Asian Pacific American students in the Law School.

name: Michigan Graduate Consulting Club
summary: We serve the Michigan graduate student community who share the same passion for pursuing management consulting as their career path.

name: Alpha Kappa Psi
summary: Alpha Kappa Psi Professional Business Fraternity believes in the advancement of its members' knowledge of today's business world. Our strength lies in the strong relationships of our members and professional resources provided. Visit us: akpsi-phi.com

name: Black Student Union
summary: The purpose of the BSU is to promote and sustain an atmosphere that is conducive to the political, cultural, social, spiritual, economic, and educational growth of students of African descent and the University community at-large.

name: Women in Mathematics
summary: The Women in Mathematics Club seeks to provide academic, career, and social support for students studying or interested in mathematics, statistics, or data science.

name: Hispanic Business Students Association
summary: The Hispanic Business Students Association provides academic support and professional development opportunities to US-based Hispanics at the Ross School of Business. We are committed to the recruitment of prospective Hispanics interested in business.

name: Michigan Concrete Canoe Team
summary: The Michigan Concrete Canoe team works together to design, fabricate, and race a canoe made from concrete as part of the annual ASCE National Concrete Canoe Competition.

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers
summary: SHPE empowers the Hispanic community to realize their fullest potential and impacts the world through STEM awareness, access, support and development.

name: Tau Beta Pi - Michigan Gamma Chapter
summary: Tau Beta Pi (TBP) is the national engineering honor society for all majors. The Michigan Gamma chapter of TBP is committed to integrity and excellence in engineering, serving the community, and promoting a spirit of liberal culture within the CoE.

name: M-Fly
summary: The goal of M-Fly is to design, build, test, and fly aircraft in a collaborative, hands-on learning environment. We compete each year in the SAE International Aero Design Competition and the SUAS Autonomous Competition.

name: Women Law Students Association
summary: WLSA supports the academic and professional growth of women and non-binary people at Michigan Law School. WLSA is committed to creating a law school community centered on the pillars of Scholarship, Leadership, Mentorship, Community, and Equitability.

name: A'Subuhi Multicultural Council
summary: Our primary mission is to provide awareness and education about multiculturalism, diversity, and social justice to all residents of West Quad. We strive to make sure that everyone is included in our residential community.

name: National Council of Negro Women at The University of Michigan
summary: The National Council of Negro Women, Inc. (NCNW) aims to lead, advocate, and empower women of African descent and their families.

name: Film and Video Student Association
summary: FVSA aims to bring together students who love to watch and make movies. We hold screenings, social events, and career events. Lightworks, a student film festival at the end of the Fall and Winter semesters, is our biggest event.

name: American Civil Liberties Union at the University of Michigan Law School Student Chapter
summary: ACLU @ MLaw is a student-led organization committed to defending and advancing civil liberties and civil rights through education, advocacy, and community-building.

name: Protect Life at the University of Michigan
summary: We are the leading pro-life group on campus. We are devoted to having conversations with students about life issues, educating our campus, and offering our support to pregnant and parenting students so they are empowered to choose life in their lives.

name: Undergraduate English Association
summary: An academic and social supplement to the English curriculum. We meet every other Wednesday afternoon in the Hopwood Library (1176 Angell Hall) at 5:30pm!

name: Alpha Pi Mu - Industrial Engineering Honor Society
summary: Alpha Pi Mu is the only nationally recognized Industrial Engineering honor society. Its purpose is to recognize high-achieving students and provide them with a more well-rounded education through professional development, networking, and other events.

name: Community of Physicists for Inclusion and Equity
summary: Our mission is to establish a community of support for underrepresented groups in physics, including women and other gender minorities, and encourage youth participation in science.  New members of any career level welcome!

name: Rackham Student Government
summary: Rackham Student Government is the student government that represents the 9,000+ graduate students on campus. Please visit our website, rsg.umich.edu for more information on our advocacy activities, funding, and events or follow us on social media.

name: Michigan Journal of International Affairs
summary: The Michigan Journal of International Affairs is a student-run, written, and edited publication. We publish op-ed pieces about geopolitics, culture, economics and more in an edition released at the end of each semester.

name: MRacing Formula SAE
summary: The University of Michigan - Ann Arbor's Formula SAE Team

name: Black Welcome Week
summary: The purpose of Black Welcome Week is to create a welcoming environment for the incoming minority freshmen as well as for the upperclassmen. It allows various campus organizations to collaborate and develop events that will be conducive to students

name: Design Movement
summary: The Design Movement creates a safe space for those interested in South Asian community to explore the person and teachings of Jesus Christ.

name: BLUElab Sa' Nima' Collaborative
summary: We are a project team focused on creating sustainable, socially engaged engineering solutions to household problems in in San Cristóbal, Guatemala.

name: Multicultural Youth Striving to Incorporate Cohesiveness
summary: MYSTIC is a multicultural council that strives to initiate community building programming, as well as social and cultural events for Alice Lloyd residents, in order to foster increased social and cultural awareness and acceptance within our community.

name: Korean International Student Association
summary: The Korean International Student Association (KISA) is an organization that promotes the unity and well-being of Korean students at the University of Michigan.

name: Camp Kesem at the University of Michigan
summary: Camp Kesem puts on a summer camp for kids whose parents have (or had) cancer, free of charge. Our volunteers fundraise throughout the year and serve as counselors at camp, directly interacting with our campers, and creating a fun-filled atmosphere.

name: Xylem Literary Magazine
summary: Xylem Literary Magazine is an independent, student-run journal that annually publishes original undergraduate student writing and art, including poetry, fiction, creative nonfiction, artwork and photography.

name: Indian American Student Association
summary: IASA serves to promote various aspects of Indian and South Asian American culture and heritage through an assortment of events that raise social, and cultural awareness, as well as fulfill goals aimed at community building and community service.

name: Social Work Doctoral Student Organization
summary: DSO is a student group for Joint Doctoral Program in Social Work and Social Science students. It acts as a liaison between doctoral students and the several supervising committees within the school of social work.

name: American Institute of Chemical Engineers
summary: The student chapter of the American Institute of Chemical Engineers at the University of Michigan encourages the professional development of its members by familiarizing students with industry, and providing social and extracurricular opportunities.

name: M-HEAL
summary: M-HEAL is a global health innovation organization that hosts robust interdisciplinary project teams to develop sustainable healthcare technology to improve health outcomes in diverse communities. Please check out our website to learn more!

name: AMERICAN RED CROSS CLUB
summary: The American Red Cross Club is devoted to furthering the mission of the American Red Cross, an organization that provides relief to victims of disasters, encourages blood donation, and helps people prevent, prepare for, and respond to emergencies.

name: BrainsRule!
summary: BrainsRule! promotes interest in science among kids by teaching them about the brain and nervous system via interactive demonstrations.

name: MUSIC Matters
summary: MUSIC Matters is a student-run non-profit organization that aims to use the unifying power of music to drive social impact in our community.

name: Noir Runway Fashion
summary: NOiR is a community service-based fashion organization at the University of Michigan, Ann Arbor. We spread campus awareness of diversity and inclusion, showcase fashion styles, and create a positive impact on the local community through philanthropy.

name: Opportunities to Educate Children
summary: Opportunities to Educate Children (OEC) seeks to promote and provide educational access for children in the Ann Arbor area by means of weekly tutoring and mentoring from University of Michigan students. Visit oec.studentorgs.umich.edu for more info.

name: Alpha Chi Sigma
summary: Alpha Chi Sigma is a co-ed professional chemistry fraternity. It strives to promote the sciences on campus and wider communities, while committing to professional development, community service, leadership and social bonding.

name: American Society of Civil Engineers
summary: The American Society of Civil Engineers chapter at the University of Michigan gives opportunities to students to network and become leaders in the civil and environmental engineering field.

name: The Urban Planning Student Association
summary: To explore, promote and support academic understanding, professional development, and the overall advancement of the field of Urban and Regional Planning.

name: Out in Public
summary: Out in Public is the Ford School's LGBTQ+ student organization committed to convening professional and academic activities that support queer and allied students of public policy.

name: Human Capital Club
summary: The Human Capital Club (HCC) promotes the understanding and integration of human capital and other organizational strategy issues in business practice through education, professional development, and networking.

name: Michigan Performance Outreach Workshop
summary: The Michigan Performance Outreach Workshop strives to share the educational power of the performing arts in order to inspire the youth of the Southeast Michigan community to pursue their own unique and life-long relationships with the arts.

name: Students for Clean Energy
summary: Students for Clean Energy (SfCE) is dedicated to increasing awareness, knowledge, and usage of renewable energy at U-M. We fulfill our mission through advocacy, campus projects, outreach, and professional development.

name: University Activities Center
summary: UAC is composed of eighteen student committees whose common goal is to provide the very best in entertainment and extra-curricular enrichment at the University of Michigan.

name: Michigan Business & Entrepreneurial Law Review
summary: The Journal's mission is to serve as a vessel for practitioner and scholarly work, including regulatory, securities, corporate, tax, intellectual property, and other legal issues involved with private equity and venture capital.

name: Entertainment, Media, and Arts Law Students Association
summary: EMALSA is a student-run organization that strives to provide events for the University of Michigan Law School community to explore opportunities within the entertainment, media, and art law fields.

name: Eta Kappa Nu
summary: An honor society for students in the Electrical Engineering and Computer Science Department. We manage the dB Café in the EECS building, contribute to community service projects, and facilitate social and networking opportunities for our members.

name: SGI Buddhist Club
summary: SGI Buddhist club seeks to contribute to peace, culture and education based on the philosophy and ideals of the Buddhism of Nichiren Daishonin as shared by the Soka Gakkai International.

name: Alternative Investments Club
summary: The Alternative Investments Club (AIC) seeks to expose students to alternative asset classes such as hedge funds, private equity, real estate and venture capital through interactive competitions, an investment fund, and events with finance professionals.

name: Women and Gender in Public Policy
summary: Women and Gender in Public Policy is a student organization at the Gerald R. Ford School of Public Policy that is committed to addressing gender issues in domestic and international policy contexts.

name: Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc. PHI Chapter
summary: Fraternity organization that seeks to unify the community with the presentation of programs and events, and community service, and bring together men who are of high character and academic achievement.

name: Michigan Technology Law Review
summary: MTLR is a law journal which uses interactive media to promote informed discourse about the interrelated legal, social, business, and public policy issues raised by emerging technologies.

name: BLUElab Woven Wind
summary: Woven Wind is a project team committed to learning and teaching about wind energy, sustainability, and engineering. We work with students around the US to design, build, and install small scale wind turbines that promote and enhance STEM education.

name: NMDP at the University of Michigan
summary: The National Marrow Donor Program (NMDP) is a global nonprofit that works to cure blood cancers and disorders. As the UofM chapter, we run events to grow NMDP's registry of tentative donors. Learn more at nmdp.org

name: GradTONES
summary: GradTONES A Cappella is a co-ed singing group composed entirely of graduate students currently enrolled at the University of Michigan. The latter half of this name is an acronym standing for "Troupe Of Needlessly Educated Singers".

name: Wolverine Capital Investments
summary: Wolverine Capital Investments is a philanthropic undergraduate investing organization that seeks to educate members about finance through active portfolio management while also serving the Ann Arbor community. WCI is a 501(c)(4) nonprofit organization.

name: Delta Sigma Pi
summary: Delta Sigma Pi is a professional business fraternity.

name: Institute of Industrial and Systems Engineers
summary: The Institute of Industrial & Systems Engineers strives to provide an awareness of the Industrial and Operations Department and its resources. IISE works to develop interdepartmental relationships as well as present insight into career opportunities.

name: American Advertising Federation
summary: We believe the best advertising isn’t advertising, it’s cultural production. Therefore, we approach all our work through a cultural lens. Each year, we create ad campaigns for NSAC as the UM chapter of the American Advertising Federation (AAF).

name: Sigma Gamma Tau
summary: Sigma Gamma Tau is the honor society for Aerospace Engineering. It seeks to identify and recognize achievement and excellence in the Aerospace field. SGT's collegiate chapters elect semiannually to grant membership to students, alumni, and professionals.

name: University of Michigan Men's Glee Club
summary: Founded in 1859, The University of Michigan Men's Glee Club is one of the oldest collegiate choirs in the nation. The club consists of male and non-binary individuals and its student leadership is responsible for all non-musical aspects of the club.

name: International Law Society
summary: The International Law Society (ILS) is devoted to promoting a greater understanding of international and comparative law and the role they play in the world today. ILS sponsors a variety of events throughout the year aimed at developing this mission.

name: Graduate Society of Women Engineers
summary: GradSWE hosts events to help female graduate engineers thrive socially, academically, and professionally. For more information, visit our website: https://gradsweatum.wixsite.com/gradswe

name: First-Generation College Students @ Michigan
summary: First-Generation College Students @ Michigan is a student organization dedicated to recognizing, raising awareness of, and resolving the needs of undergraduate first-generation college students on the University of Michigan's campus.

name: University of Michigan Supermileage Team
summary: Supermileage is a student-led multidisciplinary project team creating high efficiency single occupant electric vehicles and internal combustion vehicles to compete in the annual Shell Eco-Marathon Americas event.

name: Engineering Student Government
summary: Engineering Student Government handles funding, programming, and represents the College of Engineering student body.

name: 1931E Honor Society
summary: The 1931E Honor Society was established to bring together past and present recipients of the Class of ‘31E Scholarship so that they can grow together in fellowship and appreciation of the opportunity they have been given.

name: Gender Violence Project
summary: Gender Violence Project is a Michigan Law student organization dedicated to advocating for victims of gender-based violence.

name: Chemical Engineering Graduate Society
summary: This is a group of graduate students in the Chemical Engineering department. This group plans social, outreach, and professional development opportunities for chemical engineering graduate students, post-docs, faculty, and staff.

name: The American Constitution Society at the University of Michigan
summary: This is the American Constitution Society at the University of Michigan. ACS is a national network of progressive constitutional scholars and attorneys.

name: Arab Student Association
summary: ASA aims to unite the Arab community on campus by organizing socials, workshops, events, and professional development opportunities. We are committed to educating and increasing public awareness among the student body about Arab culture and heritage.

name: MECC Consulting Group
summary: MECC Consulting Group is targeted toward students who are interested in pursuing careers in management, strategy, or operations consulting.

name: Students for the Exploration and Development of Space: Chapter @ University of Michigan
summary: Students for the Exploration and Development of Space is a worldwide student-run organization whose mission is to enable progress in space exploration and development through technical projects, outreach events and guest speakers.

name: Society for Space Law and the Law of the Sea
summary: SSLLS focuses on exploring professional opportunities for those who hold career interests in space law or law of the sea, as well as bringing speakers to campus who can discuss various aspects of both areas of law.

name: Organization of Public Interest Students
summary: OPIS is a hub for all law students who are interested in pursuing a career in public interest law. We aim to advocate for and provide better funding, networking and programming for public interest students, and community support for student's endeavors.

name: Student Actuaries at Michigan
summary: Student Actuaries at Michigan is an organization for students interested in actuarial science and related fields. The club's main focus is to leverage its resources to support the actuarial career and the professional development of its members.

name: HBHEDoc
summary: HBHEDoc supports the academic experience and professional success of the University of Michigan HBHE doctoral students through a forum that provides opportunities to dialogue, exchange resources and information, and enhance social interactions.

name: American Civil Liberties Union Undergraduate Chapter
summary: The ACLU Undergraduate Chapter is an official affiliate of the ACLU of Michigan that works to promote civil liberties on campus through education, accountability, and activism.

name: Sexual Assault Prevention and Awareness Center Bystander Intervention and Community Engagement
summary: Sexual Assault Prevention and Awareness Center Bystander Intervention and Community Engagement works to involve and advocate for those often left out of the mainstream movement to end sexual violence by creating transformative community models.

name: Michigan Jet Engine Team
summary: MJET's goal is to design, build, and test and a working supersonic afterburning turbojet engine capable of running on sustainable aviation fuels (SAFs).

name: Kappa Delta Pi
summary: Kappa Delta Pi is dedicated to providing learning & enhancement opportunities to the students and professional members throughout the University of Michigan Community.

name: Michigan Interactive Investments
summary: Founded in 1998, Michigan Interactive Investments (MII) is a highly selective, leading undergraduate investment club at the Stephen M. Ross School of Business.

name: American Association of Pharmaceutical Scientists at the University of Michigan
summary: The American Association of Pharmaceutical Scientists at the University of Michigan Student Chapter provides a local forum for graduate students and post-doctoral fellows interested in pharmaceutical sciences.

name: Materials Science & Engineering Graduate Student Council
summary: An organization dedicated to encouraging collaboration and enhancing the social environment within the grad students affiliated with the MSE department.

name: BlueLab EASE
summary: BLUElab EASE designs and implements socially engaged engineering solutions that improve quality of life for people with disabilities, both locally and internationally.

name: Michigan Academic Competitions/UAC
summary: MAC is one of the largest and most successful academic competition (quizbowl) programs in the nation. We travel to national competitions and also organize intramural tournaments and host high school tournaments.

name: Rude Mechanicals/UAC
summary: Rude Mechanicals is a student-run theatre organization that offers students of all majors an opportunity to work on a full-scale production in any capacity: acting, directing, designing, and more. Rudes strives to be an artistic outlet for everyone.

name: Out For Business
summary: The mission of Out For Business (OFB) is to build community among the lesbian, gay, bisexual and transgender members of the Ross community and to raise awareness among all faculty, staff and students of the issues facing LGBT individuals in society.

name: The Diabetes Link at UofM
summary: The Diabetes Link at UofM is the University of Michigan chapter of The Diabetes Link. Our focus on campus is to spread awareness, educate, and raise money to cure type 1 diabetes.

name: Sexual Assault Prevention and Awareness Center: Survivor Empowerment and Ally Support
summary: The Survivor Empowerment and Ally Support program is responsible for organizing and publicizing some of SAPAC’s biggest events. Undergrad and grad students network with student orgs, table at various events, and work tirelessly to further SAPAC's causes.

name: Epidemiology 890 Student Organization
summary: To support students in Epidemiology 890, Doctoral Seminar in Epidemiology, including but not limited to building a sense of professional affiliation with the field of epidemiology and provide practice in the presentation modalities of the discipline.

name: Undergraduate Psychology Society
summary: The Undergraduate Psychology Society (UPS) provides support for undergraduates studying psychology. We plan events that function to help students to learn about opportunities and develop interests in psychology.

name: Roundtable
summary: Roundtable is a nonpartisan political student org that helps students develop their political views through research, reflection, and discussion. We actively support viewpoint diversity and respectful disagreement. All are welcome!

name: Student Organization for Computer-Human Interaction
summary: SOCHI is a pro-bono, student-driven UX and technology consulting group working with businesses and other student clubs.

name: Out in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics
summary: Our mission is to provide professional development and social support for undergraduate LGBTQIA2S+ students through networking opportunities, mentorship programs and social activities. Check out our website at ostem-um.carrd.co

name: The Accounting Club
summary: Established to aid in the professional development of college students actively interested in accounting, corporate finance and other related fields.

name: Groove Percussion Group/UAC
summary: Groove is a comedic, high energy, non-traditional percussion and performance group at the University of Michigan with one goal: to rock the masses. We drum on anything and make music out of everything. (Think STOMP and Blue Man Group!)

name: Student Astronomical Society
summary: SAS educates the public and student body about astronomy, connecting students of all majors with the community through weekly meetings, outreach opportunities, and trips. We additionally support astronomy students both academically and professionally.

name: Environmental Law Society
summary: ELS provides an opportunity to learn about environmental law and to connect with other interested students.

name: Black Law Student Association at the University of Michigan Law School
summary: It shall be the purposes of BLSA: To articulate and promote the professional needs and goals of Black law students at Michigan Law and to foster professional competence among Black law students at Michigan Law.

name: BBA Marketing Club
summary: BBA Marketing Club (BBAMC) is the original and largest marketing organization at the University of Michigan's Ross School of Business. BBAMC is designed to help students learn about and excel in marketing professionally.

name: Institute of Nuclear Materials Management at the University of Michigan
summary:
INMM at the University of Michigan is interested in 
promoting the field of nuclear materials 
management through increased awareness 
and research efforts.

name: University of Michigan Steel Bridge Team
summary: Steel Bridge Team is made of undergraduate and graduate engineering students. Each year, the team designs and fabricates a 1:10 scale bridge based on a standard set of rules. The team then competes by constructing and loading the bridge at competitions.

name: Investment Management Club
summary: The purpose of the Investment Management Club at the University of Michigan is to broaden and deepen members' knowledge and understanding of investing. Members help recruit for and obtain investment management and research positions.

name: Legal Alternative Winter Breaks
summary: Legal Alternative Winter Breaks (LAW Breaks) offers service-learning experiences for Michigan Law students during the traditional Spring Break of the academic calendar.

name: Risky Business
summary: Risky Business is a musical group comprised of MBAs and MBA partners. The group plays multiple shows throughout the school year with songs that span multiple musical artists, genres, and decades.

name: Support for Incoming Black Students
summary: The SIBS program strives to increase the retention and success of black students by providing mentorship and unique opportunities for learning and engagement within the broader University of Michigan community.

name: Michigan Health Law Organization
summary: The Michigan Health Law Organization explores current legal issues that relate to health through events and programming.

name: University of Michigan Chem-E-Car
summary: The University of Michigan Chem-E-Car provides student engineers with hands-on experience on a multi-disciplinary student project team. We design, build, test, and compete with shoe-box-sized cars powered by chemical reactions.

name: University of Michigan Journal of Law Reform
summary: Publishes legal academic articles on topics involving law reform.

name: Audio Engineering Society (Student Section) at the University of Michigan
summary: The Audio Engineering Society (Student Section) at the University of Michigan is part of a international society devoted exclusively to audio technology. It unites audio engineers, creative artists, scientists and students by promoting advances in audio.

name: Michigan Undergraduate Students of Statistics
summary: MUgSS is a student-run club sponsored by the Statistics Department. We aim to provide academic and career resources for undergraduate students interested in statistics/data science and its many applications. We welcome students from all majors!

name: Michigan Autonomous Aerial Vehicles
summary: To advance the state of the art in aerial robotics at the University of Michigan by providing students with hands-on, collaborative experience in embedded programming, control theory, navigation and mapping, imaging, and composite manufacturing.

name: Stockwell Hall Council
summary: Stockwell Hall Council (SWHC), a community focused on the unique needs and interests of second year students living in residence at Stockwell Hall.

name: Cultural Heritage Law Society
summary: The Cultural Heritage Law Society is a group of University of Michigan Law School students passionate about and advocating for the preservation of cultural heritage, repatriation of cultural property, and the restitution of looted artworks.

name: Outlaws
summary: Outlaws is a social and political organization that serves the needs of the lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, queer, intersex, and asexual community at the University of Michigan Law School.

name: Net Impact Undergrad
summary: We are a club of diverse undergraduate students passionate about the intersection of business, social impact and sustainability. We hold weekly educational, professional development, and community-building events for our members.

name: Michigan Immigration and Labor Law Association
summary: The Michigan Immigration and Labor Law Association (MILLA) is a student organization at the University of Michigan Law School. We engage in advocacy, scholarship, and service projects on labor, immigration, and low-wage and immigrant worker issues.

name: MAcc Student Association
summary: The MAcc Student Association works to unify the Master of Accounting class and to ensure that the desires of the class are being met both in and out of the classroom.

name: National Security Law Society
summary: The National Security Law Society aims to foster understanding of the legal aspects and ramifications of national and international security by sponsoring speakers and programs dealing with this area of law.

name: Business Law Association
summary: The Business Law Association (BLA) was organized to help graduate students pursue academic, professional, and social opportunities arising from the nexus of law and business.

name: Society for Human Resource Management at the University of Michigan
summary: We are committed to providing University of Michigan students with the opportunity to gain knowledge and insight into the effective management of human capital in the field of Human Resource Management.

name: Net Impact @ Ross
summary: Net Impact @ Ross is a student organization that focuses on social impact and sustainable development by education, career development, networking, and curriculum development at the Ross School of Business (RSB) and the University of Michigan (UofM).

name: International Policy Students Association
summary: IPSA's mission is to promote, facilitate, sustain, and provide opportunities for the study of international policy issues at the Gerald R. Ford School of Public Policy and the University of Michigan, Ann Arbor.

name: International Youth and Students for Social Equality
summary: The IYSSE hopes to foster discussion and political mobilization against social, political and economic inequality. While our organization encourages various means of promoting equality, our program aspires towards a socialist transformation of society.

name: MBA Investment Banking Club at the Ross School of Business
summary: The Investment Banking Club (MBA) is a student-run organization that provides members interested in investment banking careers the requisite information, tools, and mentorship required to achieve their professional aspirations.

name: Latin American Business Student Association
summary: The Latin American Business Students Association is a student organization open to all graduate students who are interested in Latin cultures and communities. Our goal is to provide a stimulating and supportive network to LABSA members.

name: Indian Subcontinent Business Association
summary: The Indian Sub-Continent Business Association is a student organization that focuses on education, career development and networking primarily for students of Indian Sub-continent, i.e. the countries – Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Maldives, Pakistan, Nepal

name: Global Investments Committee
summary: Close-knit student organization focusing on the global economy and financial markets. Using the diversity of our membership base as well as the resources in the Ross School of Business, we aim at providing an unparalleled hands-on learning experience.

name: LSA Student Honor Council
summary: The LSA Student Honor Council exists to promote a culture of integrity in our academic community.  We work with faculty, students and administration to encourage those at University of Michigan to uphold community standards and do their best work.

name: Health Informatics Student Organization
summary: We’re the representative organization for the MHI program, but we’re also open to all graduate students at the University of Michigan who are interested in health informatics.

name: American Library Association Student Chapter
summary: The mission of the American Library Association Student Chapter is to promote awareness of, interest in, and involvement in libraries, librarianship, and information services among all students at the University of Michigan School of Information.

name: National Lawyers Guild at Michigan Law
summary: A group of truly progressive law students.

name: Latinx Law Students Association
summary: LLSA is a place for Latinx law students to socialize together, provide support, mentorship, and community, as well as work hard to increase awareness of Latinx issues and recruitment.

name: Basement Arts
summary: All shows are directed, designed, and performed by students, allowing them to experiment and explore all aspects of theatre. Basement Arts continues to be a playground for artists, and the premiere venue for diverse and adventurous productions!

name: Human Factors & Ergonomics Society

summary: We believe that meaningful learning happens through doing—through the sustained effort of creating something real. Together, we aim to cultivate a culture of higher engagement, professional development, and genuine connection within the UMSI community.

name: Hearts for Arts
summary: Hearts for Arts offers creative art sessions that foster joy, connection, and emotional well-being for seniors and individuals with neurodegenerative conditions. Through art, we support memory, expression, and meaningful social engagement.

name: B132 Innovation Collective
summary: B132 Innovation Collective is a student-led hub for students who want to build, test, and launch their own ventures. We connect aspiring founders with workshops, mentors, and a strong community of innovators.

name: ADHD Student Group
summary: This is a group for students to find a community and develop tools to navigate the unique challenges of college life. Students do not need an official ADHD diagnosis to join, anyone who thinks they may benefit from the group is welcome!

name: The Scalpel & The Pen
summary: A book club for pre-health students interested in learning about careers in health care through the personal narratives of health care workers in various specialties, emphasizing their lived experiences, challenges, and the human dimensions of medicine.

name: Nam Center Student Fellows
summary: The Nam Center Student Fellows (NCSF) is an organization where students serve as intercultural ambassadors for the Nam Center for Korean Studies, working to build community through outreach, scholarship research, and immersive events.

name: Girls into VC at Michigan
summary: Girls Into VC is a community advancing women in venture capital through education, speaker events, pitch comps, firm partnerships & more. Members gain knowledge, grow networks, and join a supportive, empowering space for aspiring women in VC.

name: Michigan Innovation Ventures
summary: MIV partners with startups and venture capital funds on clearly-scoped, project-based engagements. Our goal is to provide students with real-world experience, preparing them for careers in venture capital, startups, consulting, and technology.

name: Indie Filmmaking Club
summary: A filmmaking club that produces an independent, self-funded film each semester.

name: Supporting Teamwork and Running Success
summary: We believe that meaningful connection happens through shared movement—through showing up, running together, and supporting one another regardless of pace or experience.

name: Carat Cuts
summary: Carat Cuts is a monthly junk journal club where students can drop in to work on junk journaling, scrapbooking, digital journaling, collaging, and other crafts in a welcoming, low-pressure space at their own pace using their own supplies.

name: Michigan Engineering Student Advisory Board
summary: Our organization serves to provide a method for students, faculty, and staff in the University of Michigan CoE to participate in organized communication about concerns of the student body, and the climate within the college.

name: Kappa Eta Kappa Professional Engineering Fraternity
summary: Kappa Eta Kappa is a co-ed professional engineering fraternity founded in 1923, historically focused on electrical and computer engineering. All majors and grade levels are welcome.

name: Victims First Organization at the University of Michigan
summary: VictimsFirst is a network of surviving victims of mass casualty crime and trusted supporters who have first-hand experiences of the problems and re-victimization that accompany these acts when there is a lack of coordinated effort and/or understanding.

name: Kove
summary: Kove is a group of students interested in being entrepreneurs within the coffee industry.

name: Korean Undergraduate Law Association
summary: The aim of KULA is to uplift Korean American students pursuing law, policy, and public service fields through increasing representation, professional development, professional network, and fostering a community.

name: Michigan Groundcover Student Partnership
summary: We partner with Groundcover News to take action to end homelessness and poverty. We raise awareness of Groundcover News, design and write newspaper columns, plan fundraisers, tutor and assist vendors, and volunteer at the Groundcover office.

name: Strategy Club
summary: A space for students to discuss all thing strategies, with a focus on strategic board games. Club activities also include game nights, professional development events, and social events.
